title: Power BI - Time Intelligence DAX: MoM, QoQ, YoY, Rolling Windows, Missing Periods
description: Production DAX patterns for period-over-period comparisons: MoM, QoQ, YoY (absolute and %), running totals, last-N-periods rolling windows, and handling months with no snapshot data using COALESCE and LASTNONBLANK

prerequisites.marked_date_table: All time intelligence DAX functions require a date table marked as a Date Table (Table Tools > Mark as date table). Without this, PREVIOUSMONTH, TOTALYTD, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR etc. either return errors or silently wrong results.
prerequisites.contiguous_dates: The Date column in the date table must be contiguous — no gaps. If Jan 31 is missing, PREVIOUSMONTH for March returns incorrect results.
prerequisites.base_measure:
Always define a base measure first. All period-comparison measures reference it:
VulnCount = SUM(SecuritySnapshots[Value])
// or for snapshot data that should show last known value rather than sum:
VulnCount = SUMX(SecuritySnapshots, SecuritySnapshots[Value])

mom_patterns.mom_absolute_change:
MoM Change =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _Prior   = [VulnCount PM]
RETURN
    IF(
        NOT ISBLANK(_Current) && NOT ISBLANK(_Prior),
        _Current - _Prior,
        BLANK()
    )
mom_patterns.mom_percent_change:
MoM Change % =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _Prior   = [VulnCount PM]
RETURN
    IF(
        ISBLANK(_Prior) || _Prior = 0,
        BLANK(),
        DIVIDE(_Current - _Prior, ABS(_Prior))
    )
// Note: use ABS(_Prior) when the metric can be negative (e.g. net change scores)
// Format this measure as Percentage in the Modeling pane
mom_patterns.mom_direction_flag:
// Useful for conditional formatting icons
MoM Direction =
VAR _Chg = [MoM Change]
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Chg), BLANK(),
    IF(_Chg > 0, 1,           -- 1 = increase (red for vulnerabilities)
    IF(_Chg < 0, -1, 0)))     -- -1 = decrease (green), 0 = no change
mom_patterns.previous_month_note: PREVIOUSMONTH returns ALL dates in the previous calendar month relative to the last date in the current filter context. In a matrix with months as columns, each column's filter context gives the correct previous month automatically — no looping required.

qoq_patterns.qoq_absolute:
QoQ Change =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _Prior   = [VulnCount PQ]
RETURN
    IF(NOT ISBLANK(_Current) && NOT ISBLANK(_Prior), _Current - _Prior, BLANK())
qoq_patterns.qoq_percent:
QoQ Change % =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _Prior   = [VulnCount PQ]
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Prior) || _Prior = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Current - _Prior, ABS(_Prior)))
qoq_patterns.quarter_aggregation_note: When the matrix has quarters as columns (not months), PREVIOUSQUARTER works correctly. When months are columns but you want QoQ, use DATEADD(-1, QUARTER) — it shifts the current month back by one quarter, e.g. February 2026 → November 2025.

yoy_patterns.yoy_absolute:
YoY Change =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _LY      = [VulnCount LY]
RETURN
    IF(NOT ISBLANK(_Current) && NOT ISBLANK(_LY), _Current - _LY, BLANK())
yoy_patterns.yoy_percent:
YoY Change % =
VAR _Current = [VulnCount]
VAR _LY      = [VulnCount LY]
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_LY) || _LY = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Current - _LY, ABS(_LY)))
yoy_patterns.sameperiodlastyear_vs_previousyear: SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR: shifts current period back 1 year. In a monthly matrix, March 2026 column shows March 2025 value. PREVIOUSYEAR: returns the entire prior year regardless of current period. Used for full-year vs full-year comparison in YTD measures. For month-by-month columns in a security dashboard, use SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR or DATEADD(-1, YEAR).

rolling_window_patterns.last_3_months:
// Rolling 3-month window ending at the current filter context date
VulnCount L3M =
    CALCULATE(
        [VulnCount],
        DATESINPERIOD(
            'DateTable'[Date],
            LASTDATE('DateTable'[Date]),
            -3,
            MONTH
        )
    )
rolling_window_patterns.last_12_months:
VulnCount L12M =
    CALCULATE(
        [VulnCount],
        DATESINPERIOD(
            'DateTable'[Date],
            LASTDATE('DateTable'[Date]),
            -12,
            MONTH
        )
    )
rolling_window_patterns.rolling_average:
// 3-month rolling average — used for trend smoothing
VulnCount 3M Avg =
VAR _Window = DATESINPERIOD('DateTable'[Date], LASTDATE('DateTable'[Date]), -3, MONTH)
VAR _Sum    = CALCULATE(SUM(SecuritySnapshots[Value]), _Window)
VAR _Count  = CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT('DateTable'[MonthSort]), _Window,
                         NOT ISBLANK([VulnCount]))
RETURN
    IF(_Count > 0, DIVIDE(_Sum, _Count), BLANK())
rolling_window_patterns.last_n_quarters:
// Last 4 quarters rolling
VulnCount L4Q =
    CALCULATE(
        [VulnCount],
        DATESINPERIOD(
            'DateTable'[Date],
            LASTDATE('DateTable'[Date]),
            -4,
            QUARTER
        )
    )
rolling_window_patterns.datesinperiod_note: DATESINPERIOD(dates, start_date, number_of_intervals, interval). The start_date is the anchor point; negative number_of_intervals goes backward. LASTDATE('DateTable'[Date]) within a filter context returns the last date of the current period — so in a monthly matrix column for March 2026, it returns 2026-03-31, giving the 3-month window Feb–Mar or Jan–Mar depending on the interval boundary.

missing_period_handling.problem: Security snapshot pipelines occasionally miss a month — a Logic App run fails, a CSV is not uploaded. When a month has no data in the fact table, [VulnCount] returns BLANK() for that month. Time intelligence functions treat BLANK as zero in some contexts and as 'no data' in others, producing incorrect MoM calculations.
missing_period_handling.calculate_with_lastnonblank:
// Last non-blank value up to the current period (carry-forward pattern)
VulnCount CarryForward =
VAR _LastDate =
    CALCULATE(
        LASTNONBLANK('DateTable'[Date], [VulnCount]),
        FILTER(
            ALL('DateTable'),
            'DateTable'[Date] <= MAX('DateTable'[Date])
        )
    )
RETURN
    CALCULATE([VulnCount], 'DateTable'[Date] = _LastDate)
missing_period_handling.handling_advice: For security dashboards: use BLANK() (not 0) for missing months in trend charts — gaps are visible and honest. Use COALESCE with 0 only in KPI cards where blank looks broken. For MoM calculations, check both numerator and denominator for blanks before dividing.

dateadd_reference.syntax:
DATEADD(dates, number_of_intervals, interval)
// interval: DAY, MONTH, QUARTER, YEAR
examples: DATEADD('DateTable'[Date], -1, YEAR)    -- same period last year
dateadd_reference.vs_previousmonth: DATEADD(-1, MONTH) and PREVIOUSMONTH produce identical results for month-level granularity. Prefer PREVIOUSMONTH for readability when you specifically mean 'previous month'. Use DATEADD when working with variable intervals or forward-shifting.

multi_metric_pattern.description: Security dashboards track multiple metrics simultaneously (Sev5, Sev4, External vulns, Internal vulns, Alert count). Each metric needs its own MoM/YoY calculation. The cleanest approach uses a Metric dimension with a single set of measures that adapt to the metric in context.

common_mistakes: Using PREVIOUSMONTH or SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR without a marked date table — always returns BLANK().
Applying time intelligence inside SUMX or FILTER iterators — time intelligence functions only work in CALCULATE modifier position, not in row context. Move them to CALCULATE.
Dividing by zero when prior period is zero — always check || _Prior = 0 in the DIVIDE guard.
Using DIVIDE(a - b, b) when b could be negative — use ABS(b) in the denominator for correct percentage direction.
Confusing PREVIOUSYEAR (full prior year) with SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR (same period shifted 1 year) — they differ when the current context is a partial year.

DATESINPERIOD for rolling windows returning wrong boundaries — the anchor date is LASTDATE of the context, not the start. For a '3 months ending current month' window, the anchor is the last day of the current month.
Forgetting BLANK() returns in trend line charts cause line breaks — decide consciously whether gaps (BLANK) or zeros are correct for each metric.
